metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products in the treatment of depression
metadata.document_type: Finalized Guideline (replaces CPMP/EWP/518/97 Rev.1; in effect 1 December 2013)

rules.rule_text: Major Depressive Disorder must be classified according to an internationally acknowledged classification system — preferably DSM IV-TR (preferable) or ICD-10 — using the diagnostic criteria herein; a rating scale alone is insufficient and is not equivalent to a diagnosis; the same classification system must be used throughout the whole development programme.
rules.verbatim_quote: The disorder should be classified according to an internationally acknowledged classification system, preferably DSM IV-TR (preferable) or ICD-10, using the diagnostic criteria herein. The same classification system should be used for the whole development of the medicinal product. A rating scale alone is insufficient and is not equivalent to a diagnosis.

rules.rule_text: Placebo control is required for confirmatory trials in MDD; non-inferiority alone is not an option as the sole basis for demonstrating efficacy due to the high and variable placebo response; three-arm trials including both a placebo and an active control are recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: A non-inferiority trial is not an option, as the sole basis for demonstrating efficacy. Therefore, from a scientific point of view, randomised double blind comparisons versus placebo are needed, to permit adequate evaluation of efficacy. Three-arm trials including both a placebo and an active control are recommended.
rules.exceptions: Showing superiority over an active comparator would be an acceptable alternative. Placebo is limited to approximately 6 weeks — longer durations must be justified.

rules.rule_text: At least two pivotal studies are required for the marketing authorisation application in MDD; results must be statistically significant and clinically meaningful, including responder/remitter analyses; an adequately weighted meta-analysis of efficacy across all clinical studies may support the evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the unreliability of studies in MDD at least 2 pivotal studies are required, however, the whole data package of a development program will be taken into consideration for final benefit-risk assessment. When a statistically significant effect is found and it has been shown that the effect is robust and insensitive to the analysis used, this effect has to be addressed in clinical terms (e.g. rates of responders and remitters).

rules.rule_text: Maintenance of antidepressant effect must be demonstrated in at least one randomised withdrawal study or extension study of at least 6 months duration to support the marketing authorisation.
rules.source_section: Section 4.1.2. Investigation of relapse and recurrence
rules.verbatim_quote: For authorisation it should be shown that a short-term effect can be maintained during the index episode. For this a randomised withdrawal study, allowing to study relapse prevention is probably the best design. The period after (re-) randomisation usually has a duration of 6 months.
rules.exceptions: A purely placebo-controlled extension study is not recommended as the best option; requires justification if used as the alternative.

rules.rule_text: Short-term confirmatory trials in MDD must be double-blind, randomised, parallel-group, and placebo-controlled, with study duration of approximately 6 weeks (minimum 4 weeks); dosages used must be justified; inclusion of a well-accepted standard active control as a third arm is strongly recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: Confirmatory trials should be double-blind, randomised and parallel group trials. Dosages used should be justified. The dossier should include parallel group studies against placebo and active comparator. Three-arm or multi-arm studies are strongly recommended for pivotal studies in phase III of development. The duration of these trials usually is around 6 weeks.
rules.exceptions: In some programs 8 weeks have been studied; shorter durations (at least 4 weeks) are acceptable if sufficient to separate active treatment from placebo.

rules.rule_text: Primary efficacy must be assessed with validated depression rating scales; acceptable primary scales include HAMD-17 item and MADRS; the scale used as the primary variable must be specified in the protocol; a 50% improvement on a usual rating scale is accepted as a clinically relevant responder definition in MDD.
rules.verbatim_quote: Efficacy must be assessed by rating scales. Acceptable scales for use as primary endpoint to determine symptomatic improvement include the Hamilton Rating Scale of Depression, preferably the 17 item scale, and the Montgomery Asberg Depression Rating Scale. The protocol should indicate which scale is used as primary variable. In MDD a 50% improvement of a patient on a usual rating scale is accepted as a clinically relevant response.
rules.exceptions: Other validated scales may be acceptable; criteria for response and remission must be pre-specified and justified.

rules.rule_text: Recurrence prevention is not a mandatory part of the registration package for the treatment of MDD episodes; it constitutes an additional claim and requires specific studies in patients in full remission randomised to active treatment or placebo, with pre-specified definition of recurrence as a depressive episode meeting current DSM criteria with a certain degree of severity on a validated rating scale.
rules.source_section: Section 4.1.2. Investigation of relapse and recurrence
rules.verbatim_quote: Prevention of the next episode(s) or recurrence prevention is not a mandatory part of a registration package for treatment of MDD episodes. When a claim is made, specific studies are needed. Patients in full remission should be randomized to active treatment or placebo. Recurrence should be prespecified as a depressive episode that fulfils current DSM criteria and a certain degree of severity on a validated rating scale.

rules.rule_text: For a Treatment Resistant Depression (TRD) claim, at least one additional short-term trial beyond the standard MDD development programme must be performed; TRD is defined as lack of clinically meaningful improvement despite adequate doses of at least two antidepressant agents of the appropriate class(es) for adequate duration and with affirmed treatment adherence; at least one treatment failure must be demonstrated prospectively.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.1. Trials to study monotherapy in treatment resistant patients
rules.verbatim_quote: At least one additional short term trial should be performed to support extension of the indication to treatment resistant patients. Treatment resistance in major depression is defined as lack of clinically meaningful improvement despite the use of adequate doses of at least two antidepressant agents, derived from the group(s) of commonly used first line treatment, prescribed for adequate duration with adequate affirmation of treatment adherence. At least one treatment failure should be shown prospectively.
rules.exceptions: Subgroup analyses in general-population trials are insufficient for the extended TRD indication; demonstrating superiority over placebo alone is not sufficient.

rules.rule_text: For an augmentation/add-on MDD claim, separate randomised parallel trials in partial responders are required; TRD patients who show no clinically meaningful change from baseline are not suitable candidates for augmentation and must be excluded from augmentation trials; PK/PD drug interaction studies relevant to the new compound must be completed before pivotal augmentation studies.
rules.source_section: Section 4.4.2. Trials to study augmentation/add-on treatment
rules.verbatim_quote: The use of a compound to augment the activity of another product is worth a specific claim leading to a separate indication statement. This must be substantiated by data demonstrating efficacy in short term and long term trials. Patients with TRD (who show no clinically meaningful change from baseline as result of treatment) are not suitable candidates for augmentation. Pharmakokinetic or pharmacodynamic drug interactions relevant to the specific characteristics of the new compound should be studied prior to pivotal augmentation studies.

rules.rule_text: In paediatric MDD studies, separate studies must be conducted in children and adolescents; efficacy in acute treatment must be demonstrated in at least one short-term trial of at least 8 weeks duration including a placebo and an active comparator arm; throughout the trials all subjects must receive standardised psychosocial interventions; adult efficacy and safety data must not be extrapolated to the paediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Extrapolation of adult efficacy and safety data is not considered appropriate. Specific studies are necessary in the paediatric population. Separate studies should generally be conducted in children and adolescents. Efficacy in acute treatment should be demonstrated in at least one short term trial of 8 weeks duration (or longer) including a placebo and an active comparator arm. Throughout the trials all subjects should receive psychosocial interventions; this should be standardised if possible.
rules.exceptions: If a trial includes both children and adolescents, stratification for age group must be employed with sample size calculation allowing demonstration of efficacy in each age group independently.

rules.rule_text: For paediatric MDD trials, the primary endpoint must be the change from baseline in validated, age-appropriate rating scales for the core signs and symptoms of MDD; response and remission must be defined in the protocol; global and/or functional outcome measures must be estimated as secondary endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: Primary endpoint should be the change from baseline in validated, age appropriate rating scales for the core signs and symptoms of MDD. Response and remission should be defined in the protocol. Global and/or functional outcome measures should be estimated as secondary endpoints.

rules.rule_text: For older people, defining a safe dose (range) is a main concern to be addressed before licensing; this should be done through either adequate representation of elderly in the total database (prospective subgroup analysis) or through specific elderly trials; PK studies should support dose recommendation; new products with a new mechanism of action generally require specific trials in older people.
rules.verbatim_quote: Therefore not only efficacy, but defining a safe dose (range) in these patients is a main concern. Usually this should be addressed before licensing. For new products with a new mechanism of action specific trials are usually needed. In both situations pharmacokinetic studies may support the choice of the dose and should be conducted.
rules.exceptions: The first approach (subgroup from total database) may be accepted as pivotal information for agents of known pharmacological classes, provided a reasonable number of older people is included.

rules.rule_text: Suicidal thoughts and behaviour must be actively measured using validated instruments (e.g. C-SSRS, InterSePT Scale for Suicidal Thinking, or other validated instruments) throughout all clinical trials; rates of suicidal events from suicidal ideation to completed suicide must be presented; narrative summaries of suicidal patient statements or behaviours must be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: The potential for the test product to precipitate suicidal thoughts and behaviour should be actively measured using validated rating scales (e.g. InterSePT Scale for Suicidal Thinking, Columbia Suicidality Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) or other validated instruments). Rates of suicidal events (from suicidal ideation to completed suicide) should be presented and narrative summaries of suicidal patient statements or behaviours should be provided.

rules.rule_text: Cognitive functioning must be monitored using validated rating scales; effects on cognition, reaction time, driving, and severity of sedation must be assessed; in the adolescent population, specific effects on memory, learning, and school performance must be studied.
rules.source_section: Section 4.5.3.2. Adverse effects on cognitive functioning
rules.verbatim_quote: A detrimental effect on cognition should be monitored using validated rating scales, which may be identical to those used to support an efficacy claim. Effects on cognition, reaction time, driving and severity of sedation should also be studied. In the adolescent population specific issues such as memory, learning, school performance, etc. should be studied in relation to both the safety and efficacy perspective.

rules.rule_text: QT interval prolongation must be investigated in accordance with ICH E14 guideline for antidepressant products given the known cardiovascular effects of this drug class.
rules.verbatim_quote: The effect on QT-interval prolongation should be investigated in accordance with the ICH E14 guideline.

rules.rule_text: If antipsychotics are used for augmentation or as a treatment option in TRD, extrapyramidal symptoms (EPS) must be actively assessed using validated and specifically designed rating scales; extent and severity of EPS, including tardive dyskinesia, must be measured; dose-response relationships of EPS must be explored.
rules.verbatim_quote: If antipsychotics are used for augmentation or as treatment option in treatment resistant depressive patients, rates of extrapyramidal symptoms should be presented. In addition the extent and severity of EPS should be actively measured using validated and specifically designed rating scales. Dose – response relationships of EPS should be explored. During the wash out phase prior to acute studies, possible tardive EPS should be measured to distinguish this from acute EPS due to the test treatment.
rules.exceptions: Applies specifically when antipsychotics are used in the MDD development programme.

rules.rule_text: Metabolic risk factors (weight, glucose metabolism, lipid metabolism) must be actively measured using standard laboratory measures in all antidepressant clinical trials; the metabolic profile must be thoroughly characterised in comparison with placebo and active comparator(s).
rules.verbatim_quote: The effects on weight, glucose metabolism and lipid metabolism should be actively measured using standard laboratory measures. The metabolic profile of the test product should be thoroughly characterised in comparison with placebo and active comparator(s).

rules.rule_text: Rebound and withdrawal phenomena must be studied by designing trials in which treatment is stopped abruptly with appropriate follow-up; dependent on mechanism of action, gradual tapering may be more appropriate; occurrence of rebound and/or withdrawal phenomena must be scored at the appropriate time.
rules.verbatim_quote: Trials should be designed in such a way, that these phenomena can be studied. In some of the short-term and long-term clinical trials, treatment should be stopped abruptly and patients should be followed for a suitable duration, in other studies careful tapering off might be more appropriate depending on the mechanism of action of the compound. Occurrence of rebound and/or withdrawal phenomena should be scored at the appropriate time.

rules.rule_text: In paediatric and adolescent safety evaluation, active monitoring (not spontaneous AE reporting) using validated questionnaires and scales must be employed for potential treatment-emergent adverse events including somnolence, sexual disturbances, weight gain, suicidality, and discontinuation/rebound symptoms.
rules.verbatim_quote: Rather than relying on spontaneous AE reporting, potential treatment-emergent adverse events such as somnolence, sexual disturbances, weight gain, affective symptoms such as suicidality, discontinuation/rebound symptoms, etc. should be clearly defined and actively monitored for. Validated questionnaires/scales/tests should be used for the assessment of adverse events.

rules.rule_text: Haematological adverse events must receive special attention in antidepressant clinical programmes; particular focus is required on the incidence of neutropenia, agranulocytosis, and aplastic anaemia.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special attention should be paid to incidence of neutropenia, agranulocytosis and aplastic anaemia.
